_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (Opus 4.8)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX House HD129-133 (Houston/Harris) batch: dennis-paul (R, running for TX Senate SD11), tom-oliverson (R, House Majority Leader), alma-allen (D, RETIRING), mike-schofield (R, swing dist), mano-deayala (R). Evidence-first; every TRUE/FALSE cited; null where no evidence.

set.office: Texas State Representative, HD129 (sitting; 2026 GOP nominee for TX Senate SD11)

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified June 2026: still sitting HD129 rep through Jan 2027; won GOP nomination for open TX Senate SD11 (no runoff), faces Democrat Shannon Dicely in the Nov 2026 general. NOT seeking HD129 re-election.

dennis-paul@TX.notes_set: Republican, HD129 (Clear Lake/SE Harris). Won the GOP nomination for TX Senate SD11 (Middleton's open seat) on 3/3/2026; faces Shannon Dicely (D) in Nov 2026 (running for higher office). Strong social-conservative voting record: Texas Alliance for Life 89th-Leg. 2025 = 93%; Texas Values Action = 84%. DEVIATION: Texas Scorecard Fiscal Responsibility Index career grade F (fiscal-moderate, not a hardliner).

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 89th-Leg. 2025 scorecard 93%; voted FOR SB33 (no taxpayer funds for abortion).

2.note: Voted FOR SB8 (Women's Privacy Act), HB229 (What is a Woman Act), and SB1257 (Detransitioners Bill of Rights), 2025 — affirms biological sex.
Voted FOR SB13 (2025) banning harmful/sexually-explicit books in school libraries.

0.note: Voted FOR SB965 (2025) 'Coach Joe Kennedy Law' protecting school-staff religious expression, and SB11 school prayer.

3.note: Voted FOR SB10 (Ten Commandments in schools, 3rd reading) and SB11 (school prayer), 2025.

2.note: Texas Scorecard Fiscal Responsibility Index career grade F (2025 score 82) — documented fiscal-moderate record, not fiscal-restraint hardliner.

4.note: Authored HB302 (2021) barring landlords from prohibiting lawful firearm possession by tenants — opposes citizen disarmament.

set.office: Texas State Representative, HD130 (Texas House Majority Leader / GOP Caucus Chair)

profile.religion: Baptist (member, Jersey Village Baptist Church, Houston)
profile.background: Anesthesiologist (MD, Baylor College of Medicine); Texas House Majority Leader and Republican Caucus Chair.
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified June 2026: won 3/3/2026 GOP primary (100%, unopposed); faces Brett Robinson (D) in Nov 2026 general.

tom-oliverson@TX.notes_set: Republican, HD130 (NW Harris/Cypress). House Majority Leader & GOP Caucus Chair; won 2026 primary unopposed, faces Brett Robinson (D) in November. Documented faith-family/life hardliner: Texas Values Action 100% 'Faith & Family Champion'; Texas Alliance for Life 89th-Leg. 2025 = 89%; floor advocate for SB14 (2023) and sponsor of HB1686/HB3817. DEVIATION (noted, not scored): Texas Scorecard Fiscal Index F in 2017/2021/2023 (improved to 84 in 2025).

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 89th-Leg. 2025 scorecard 89%; recognized pro-life leader; helped secure ~$200M for pro-life providers; voted SB8, SB33 (2025).

2.note: Spoke FOR SB14 (2023 minor gender-care ban) on the House floor; sponsored HB1686 (2023) and HB3817 (2025, gender on government records); voted HB229, SB1257 (2025).

4.note: Sponsored HB3817 (gender on government records); voted AGAINST HB1738 repeal of the homosexual-conduct statute (i.e., to keep it) — opposes LGBTQ promotion in public policy.

0.note: As GOP Caucus Chair voted FOR and helped lead the SB2 (2025) universal ESA/school-choice effort.

1.note: Voted FOR SB12 (2025) Parents' Bill of Rights.

2.note: Voted FOR SB13 (2025) banning harmful books in school libraries.

0.note: Voted FOR SB11 (2025) school prayer; member Jersey Village Baptist Church; publicly attributes his public service to a Christian calling.

3.note: Voted FOR SB10 (Ten Commandments in schools) and SB11 (school prayer), 2025.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified June 2026: announced 9/2/2025 she will NOT seek re-election (retiring at age 86); finishes current term through Jan 2027. Seat open; Staci Childs won the Democratic runoff to succeed her.

alma-allen@TX.notes_set: Democrat, HD131 (SW Houston); 11-term incumbent, longtime public educator/former SBOE member. RETIRING — did not file for the 3/3/2026 primary; seat open (Staci Childs won the D runoff). Documented progressive record: pro-equality (Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus endorsement); intended NO on SB14 (House Journal statement of vote); voted NO on SB2 vouchers; joined the 2021 quorum walkout against SB1; opposed SB4 (2017). NOTE: a JBS 'Freedom Index' that appears to show conservative votes is a recorded-vote/framing artifact (e.g., her SB14 'Yea' was formally disavowed) — not scored.

0.note: Endorsed by the Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus as a pro-equality candidate (2024) — does not affirm one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: Pro-equality (LGBTQ+ Caucus endorsement) — does not oppose same-sex marriage/civil unions.

2.note: On SB14 (2023 minor gender-care ban) recorded Yea but filed a House Journal statement of vote: 'I intended to vote no' — documented intent to oppose.

4.note: Endorsed by the Houston LGBTQ+ Political Caucus as pro-equality (2024).

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025) school-choice/ESA. 40-year public educator and Texas AFT 'champion for public education.'

1.note: Joined the 2021 Texas House Democratic quorum break to block SB1, the GOP voter law that added ID/citizenship-related requirements; opposed SB1.

3.note: 2021 quorum walkout opposed SB1's restrictions on mail-in voting and drop boxes.

2.note: Signed a 2017 public letter opposing SB4 (sanctuary-cities ban), calling it unlawful targeting of immigrant communities.

profile.confidence_note: Web-verified June 2026: won the 3/3/2026 GOP primary; faces Sara McGee (D) in the Nov 2026 general. HD132 (Katy/west Harris) is a competitive swing seat (he lost it in 2018, regained it in 2020).

mike-schofield@TX.notes_set: Republican, HD132 (Katy/west Harris — swing district). Won 2026 primary; faces Sara McGee (D) in November. Strong on life (Texas Alliance for Life 89th-Leg. 2025 = 96%) and held the line on school choice (voted YES on SB2). DEVIATION: Texas Scorecard Fiscal Responsibility Index F grades in 2021 and 2023 (career C+; 2025 = 79) — fiscal-moderate record, though he authored the 2019 constitutional income-tax ban. Marriage/Christian-liberty/election/justice/federalism/border/2A cells left null — prior all-TRUE marks were uncited and could not be verified.

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 (2025) ESA/school-choice; the Texas Tribune tracker corrected his record from 'absent' to 'yes.'

2.note: Texas Scorecard Fiscal Responsibility Index F grades in 2021 and 2023 (career C+; 2025 score 79) — documented fiscal-moderate record. (Counter-credit: authored the 2019 constitutional income-tax ban.)

profile.background: Attorney; served on the Texas Board of Criminal Justice 2017-2023 (chair, Business & Finance Committee).
profile.confidence_note: Web-verified June 2026: won the 3/3/2026 GOP primary uncontested (100%); faces Josh Wallenstein (D) in the Nov 2026 general.

mano-deayala@TX.notes_set: Republican, HD133 (west Houston/Memorial). Won 2026 primary uncontested; faces Josh Wallenstein (D) in November. Solidly conservative cultural/life record: Texas Alliance for Life 89th-Leg. 2025 = 100%; SB14 (2023) co-sponsor; SB2 voucher YES; campaign-attested co-authorship of SB15 (women's sports), HB900 (READER Act), SB833 (anti-ESG), HB6/HB800/SB1900 (crime/cartels). DEVIATIONS (establishment-leaning, not progressive): voted to IMPEACH AG Ken Paxton (2023), and a fiscal-moderate 57/100 on the Texans for Fiscal Responsibility Index. Prior record's '60 vs 100' header inconsistency and a mis-cited Tennessee rifle PAC / chronologically-impossible constitutional-carry claim are cleared by this finalization (he took office in 2023, after 2021 permitless carry).

2.note: Co-sponsor of SB14 (2023) ban on gender-modification procedures for minors; states co-authorship of SB15 Save Women's Sports Act.

0.note: Voted YES on SB2 (2025) ESA/school-choice (only two House Republicans voted no; he was not one).

2.note: States co-authorship of HB900 (2023) READER Act removing sexually explicit material from school libraries.
Texans for Fiscal Responsibility Index 57/100 (88th, 2023) — fiscal-moderate; flagged for corporate-welfare and anti-transparency votes and Speaker Phelan support.

4.note: States co-authorship of SB833 (2023) barring ESG-based insurance ratemaking.

2.note: Authored/sponsored HB6 & HB800 (human-trafficking and fentanyl penalty enhancements) and SB1900 (cartels as foreign terrorist orgs); served on the Texas Board of Criminal Justice 2017-2023 — proportional punishment of evildoers.

0.note: States he helped appropriate $5.1B to border security (Operation Lone Star military/border presence).
